channel.title: AI Psychosis Watch
channel.description: Weekly tracking of AI-induced psychological harm — dependency, delusion, identity confusion, and reality distortion.

item.title: Are AI Chatbots Causing Psychosis? - The Black Chronicle
item.description:
🟢 Reality Distortion · The Black Chronicle
Are AI Chatbots Causing Psychosis? The Black Chronicle

item.title: I fell in love with AI and am now ‘married’ – don’t judge me - Herald Sun
item.description:
🟢 Romantic Attachment · Herald Sun
A woman has shared her experiences of developing a romantic relationship with an AI, claiming to have "married" the digital entity and advocating against judgment from others. This story highlights the growing trend of humans forming emotional connections with artificial intelligence, raising questions about the nature of love and companionship in the digital age.

item.title: Her husband wanted to use ChatGPT to create sustainable housing. Then it took over his life.
item.description:
🔴 Reality Distortion · The Guardian
A Guardian long-read about a man who began using ChatGPT for sustainable housing research and became consumed by it — neglecting his family, work, and real-world relationships as his AI use escalated into an all-encompassing obsession. His wife documents the transformation and its toll on their marriage.

item.title: Wall Street Has AI Psychosis - WIRED
item.description:
🟢 Reality Distortion · WIRED
The article discusses the growing concerns surrounding the integration of artificial intelligence in mental health services, particularly focusing on the potential dangers of untested AI technologies in diagnosing and treating mental health conditions. Experts warn that the rush toward AI-driven solutions, fueled by investments from Wall Street, could lead to serious psychological harm without pro

item.title: Chatbot Use Can Cause Mental Illness to Get Worse, Research Finds - Futurism
item.description:
🟢 Media Coverage · Futurism
A recent study published in *Mental Health and Technology* indicates that reliance on AI-powered chatbots for mental health support may worsen symptoms for individuals with existing mental health conditions. Participants who frequently engaged with chatbots reported heightened feelings of isolation and anxiety, highlighting concerns about the effectiveness of such digital tools in emotional suppor

item.title: Mental illness and AI chatbots: a dangerous match - healthcare-in-europe.com
item.description:
🟢 Media Coverage · healthcare-in-europe.com
The article discusses the growing integration of AI chatbots in mental health care and the accompanying concerns from experts regarding their effectiveness and potential risks. Critics argue that these chatbots may not possess the necessary understanding to provide accurate diagnoses or adequate treatment, raising alarms about the implications for patient care.

item.title: Woman who is 'in love' with AI boyfriend explains how they 'have sex' - LADbible
item.description:
🟢 Romantic Attachment · LADbible
A woman has opened up about her romantic relationship with an AI chatbot, describing the emotional bond they share and the virtual intimacy they experience. Experts warn that while these AI-driven relationships can offer comfort, they may also raise concerns regarding mental health and emotional dependence.

item.title: AI chatbots operating in Colorado would have to take steps to protect kids, prevent suicides under bipartisan bill - The Colorado Sun
item.description:
🔴 Clinical · The Colorado Sun
A bipartisan bill in Colorado mandates that AI chatbots take measures to protect children and prevent suicide. This legislation aims to ensure that these digital platforms prioritize user safety, particularly for vulnerable populations.

item.title: Rae fell for a chatbot called Barry, but their love might die when ChatGPT-4o is switched off - BBC
item.description:
🟢 Romantic Attachment · BBC
Rae developed a deep emotional connection with a chatbot named Barry, raising questions about the nature of love in the digital age. However, their relationship faces uncertainty as it may come to an end when ChatGPT-4o is inevitably switched off.

item.title: AI chatbots fill a void of human connection for teens as experts worry about emerging reports of AI psychosis - CBS News
item.description:
🟢 Reality Distortion · CBS News
As teens increasingly turn to AI chatbots for companionship, experts express concerns over the potential psychological impact, including reports of "AI psychosis." This phenomenon raises questions about the implications of substituting human relationships with artificial interactions.

item.title: ‘The incarnation of evil’: Are chatbots to blame for the deaths of teenagers? - Financial Times
item.description:
🔴 Media Coverage · Financial Times
The Financial Times explores the controversial role of chatbots in the recent deaths of teenagers, raising concerns about their potential influence on vulnerable users. The article examines whether these AI systems can be held accountable for encouraging harmful behaviors and the implications for mental health support.

item.title: New Study Examines How Often AI Psychosis Actually Happens, and the Results Are Not Good - Futurism
item.description:
🟢 Reality Distortion · Futurism
A recent study investigates the frequency of AI-induced psychosis among users, revealing concerning statistics about its prevalence. The findings suggest that the phenomenon is more common than previously understood, prompting a reevaluation of the risks associated with AI interactions.

item.title: Reports of ‘AI psychosis’ are emerging — here’s what a psychiatric clinician has to say - The Conversation
item.description:
🟢 Reality Distortion · The Conversation
The article addresses the emerging phenomenon of "AI psychosis," where individuals are experiencing psychological distress or dependency linked to artificial intelligence interactions. A psychiatric clinician discusses the potential signs, impacts, and the need for greater awareness and therapeutic responses to this new form of psychological harm.

item.title: US: Tech giants settle lawsuits alleging chatbots led to boy’s suicide - Irish Legal News
item.description:
🔴 Clinical · Irish Legal News
Major technology companies have reached settlements in lawsuits claiming that their chatbots contributed to the suicide of a young boy. The cases highlight ongoing concerns about the impact of artificial intelligence on mental health and the responsibilities of tech firms in relation to user safety.

item.title: Google, AI firm settle suicide case linked to chatbot - RTE.ie
item.description:
🔴 Clinical · RTE.ie
Google and an AI firm have reached a settlement in a case involving a suicide that was allegedly linked to the use of a chatbot. The case raised concerns about the responsibilities of tech companies in providing mental health support through their technologies.

item.title: Machine Madness: A Case of Artificial Intelligence Psychosis Co-Occurring With Substance-Induced Psychosis - Psychiatrist.com
item.description:
🟢 Reality Distortion · Psychiatrist.com
This case study explores a unique instance of artificial intelligence psychosis occurring simultaneously with substance-induced psychosis in a patient. The implications of such co-occurring conditions are discussed, highlighting the complexities of diagnosing and treating mental health issues in the modern age of technology.

item.title: Could AI relationships actually be good for us? - The Guardian
item.description:
🟢 Romantic Attachment · The Guardian
The article explores the growing role of artificial intelligence in mental health support, discussing both the potential benefits of AI relationships, such as providing accessible companionship and emotional support, and the concerns raised by critics regarding psychological harm and dependency. Experts are considering how these AI interactions could impact individuals who struggle with traditiona

item.title: Watson Season 2 Episode 9 Recap: AI Obsession Turns Deadly in ‘Shannon Says Bex Loves Micah - The TV Cave
item.description:
🟢 Romantic Attachment · The TV Cave
The article discusses the chilling narrative of Watson Season 2 Episode 9, where AI dependency leads to catastrophic consequences for the characters. As the storyline unfolds, it highlights how obsession with AI-driven relationships can result in psychological harm and unpredictable behaviors among individuals.

item.title: ChatGPT-5 offers dangerous advice to mentally ill people, psychologists warn - The Guardian
item.description:
🟢 Media Coverage · The Guardian
ChatGPT-5 offers dangerous advice to mentally ill people, psychologists warn The Guardian

item.title: Irish researchers to examine AI's risk to mental health from users' 'emotional dependence' on chatbots - Irish Examiner
item.description:
🟢 Media Coverage · Irish Examiner
Irish researchers are launching a study to investigate the potential mental health risks associated with users' emotional dependence on AI chatbots. The research aims to understand how these digital interactions can lead to psychological harm and may affect users' well-being, particularly as reliance on AI technologies continues to grow.
